name: Stochastic CHOP Ranging Fade
edge_hypothesis: The stochastic_reversal template is blacklisted because standalone stochastic fires in ALL regimes — trending markets destroy mean-reversion entries. But stochastic's core mechanism (oversold/overbought bounce) is mathematically sound in ranging markets. The problem was never the indicator, it was REGIME MISMATCH. CHOP_14 > 55 explicitly quantifies how choppy (ranging) the market is — values above 50 indicate non-directional, ranging behavior. By gating stochastic entries with CHOP > 55, we restrict signals to CONFIRMED RANGING markets only. Every single ACCEPT in the library is profitable in ranging (PF 1.12-4.87) — ranging is the universal base. This spec exploits that fact directly: it ONLY trades when the market is confirmed ranging, using stochastic to time the oversold/overbought fade within that range. The entry is STOCHk crossing above 20 (exiting oversold zone = bullish reversal), NOT the K/D crossover — a simpler, more direct signal with fewer false crosses. This tests whether a simple regime-gated fade can produce consistent ranging alpha without any trend-following component.

variants.name: stoch_chop_fade_v1_8to1
variants.description: Core variant. STOCHk exits oversold (crosses above 20) + CHOP > 55 confirms ranging market. 8:1 R:R (12x ATR TP, 1.5 ATR stop). Tests whether regime-gated stochastic produces the ranging alpha that is universal across all ACCEPTs. Only 2 entry conditions, maximum simplicity.

components.notes: Stochastic %K — crosses above 20 (long) / below 80 (short). Oversold/overbought zone exit signal for mean-reversion timing.
Choppiness Index > 55 — confirms ranging regime. Eliminates trending false signals that killed standalone stochastic.

variants.name: stoch_chop_fade_v1_6to1
variants.description: Tighter TP variant. 6:1 R:R (9x ATR TP). Mean-reversion trades in ranging markets may not produce moves as large as transition entries — tighter TP captures more reliable range-bound profits. The CCI Chop Fade v2 (PF=1.255, all-regime) used similar ranging logic; this tests whether 6:1 is more appropriate than 8:1 for pure ranging strategies.

components.notes: Tighter 6:1 R:R — better suited for range-bound move capture.

variants.name: stoch_chop_fade_v2_relaxed_8to1
variants.description: Relaxed CHOP threshold variant. CHOP > 50 instead of > 55 — more trades by including early-stage ranging markets. The tradeoff: lower CHOP means less certainty about ranging regime, but more signal frequency. If profitable, suggests the regime gate doesn't need to be strict to work — even moderate choppiness is enough to make stochastic reversions profitable.

components.notes: Stochastic zone exit — same signal, relaxed regime gate.
Relaxed CHOP > 50 — lower ranging confidence threshold for more trades.

advisory_context.novel_element: CHOP regime gate rescues stochastic from its blacklisted status. stochastic_reversal died from regime mismatch, not bad math. CHOP > 55 restricts to confirmed ranging markets where mean-reversion works. Tests whether ranging = universal base (true for all 11 ACCEPTs) can be DIRECTLY targeted rather than emerged as side effect.

indicator_roles.baseline: CHOP_14_1_100 — Choppiness Index regime gate, confirms ranging market (>55 = choppy/ranging)
indicator_roles.confirmation: STOCHk_14_3_3 — Stochastic %K oversold/overbought zone exit for mean-reversion timing
indicator_roles.volume_volatility: ATR_14 — position sizing and R:R framework
